articles.title: India Weather Alert: North India to See 40°C Heat, South to See Rain Relief - Full Forecast For April 16
articles.full_text:
As the weather patterns shift across the country, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a detailed forecast for April 16 (Thursday), outlining significant changes in the weather across various regions of India. While North India is expected to see soaring temperatures, exceeding 40 degrees Celsius, South regions, including Kerala is likely to see scattered showers.
North India Weather Forecast
North India is bracing for substantial weather changes as two Western Disturbances approach the region. The first disturbance is expected to impact areas between April 16 and 17, followed closely by a second system between April 18 and 19. Major cities such as Delhi are set to experience soaring temperatures, with forecasts suggesting that the mercury may exceed 40 degrees Celsius for the first time this season.
In terms of temperatures, Chandigarh will see a maximum of 36 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 17 degrees Celsius, while New Delhi is predicted to reach a high of 39 degrees Celsius and a low of 20 degrees Celsius. Other notable temperatures include Amritsar at 34 degrees Celsius, Jammu at 32 degrees Celsius, and Dehradun at 35 degrees Celsius.
While higher regions, particularly in Himachal Pradesh and Jammu and Kashmir, may receive light to moderate rainfall and even snowfall in the mountainous areas.
South India Weather Update
In South India, particularly in Karnataka and Kerala, the weather is expected to remain relatively stable. Bengaluru will experience mild conditions with temperatures ranging from 20 degrees Celsius to 30 degrees Celsius. Meanwhile, Kerala is likely to see scattered showers, providing much-needed relief from the increasing heat.
West India Weather Update
Moving to West India, cities such as Mumbai will witness warm conditions with maximum temperatures hovering around 34 degrees Celsius. The region is expected to remain dry, with no significant weather systems forecasted. Local residents should prepare for warm afternoons, particularly as the summer progresses.
East India Weather Update
East India is also set to experience varied weather patterns. Cities like Kolkata are likely to see warm and humid conditions, with temperatures reaching up to 35 degrees Celsius. The IMD anticipates above-normal rainfall in certain pockets, contributing to fluctuating weather conditions in the region.
